channel.title: www.aihero.dev
channel.description: AI Hero - AI Engineering for Curious Professional Developers

item.title: AI Hero Newsletter by Matt Pocock
item.description: Subscribe to be the first to learn about AI Hero releases, updates, and special discounts for AI Heroes.
item.content:encoded: Subscribe to be the first to learn about AI Hero releases, updates, and special discounts for AI Heroes.

item.title: AI Skills for Real Engineers
item.description: A practical skill system for engineers who want to use AI without giving up their standards.
item.content:encoded: A practical skill system for engineers who want to use AI without giving up their standards.

item.title: Skills Newsletter
item.description: A practical skill system for engineers who want to use AI without giving up their standards.
item.content:encoded: A practical skill system for engineers who want to use AI without giving up their standards.

item.title: improve-codebase-architecture: Find Deepening Opportunities
item.description: A guide to the improve-codebase-architecture skill for finding refactors that make code easier to test, change, and navigate with agents.
item.content:encoded: A guide to the improve-codebase-architecture skill for finding refactors that make code easier to test, change, and navigate with agents.

item.title: handoff: Move Context Between Agent Sessions
item.description: A guide to the handoff skill for compacting context so another agent or session can continue cleanly.
item.content:encoded: A guide to the handoff skill for compacting context so another agent or session can continue cleanly.

item.title: prototype: Answer Questions With Throwaway Code
item.description: A guide to the prototype skill for testing UI, state, and product decisions before committing to implementation.
item.content:encoded: A guide to the prototype skill for testing UI, state, and product decisions before committing to implementation.

item.title: Skills Changelog: /handoff, /prototype, /review and /writing
item.description: Two new AI agent skills: /handoff for passing context between agents, and /prototype for building throwaway prototypes to test design decisions.
item.content:encoded: Two new AI agent skills: /handoff for passing context between agents, and /prototype for building throwaway prototypes to test design decisions.

item.title: AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The vocabulary of AI coding, translated into plain English for engineers.
item.content:encoded: The vocabulary of AI coding, translated into plain English for engineers.

item.title: Model | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The parameters. Stateless — does next-token prediction and nothing else. Cannot do anything agentic on its own.
item.content:encoded: The parameters. Stateless — does next-token prediction and nothing else. Cannot do anything agentic on its own.

item.title: Parameters | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The numbers inside a model — often billions — tuned during training. Everything the model knows lives in them. Also called weights.
item.content:encoded: The numbers inside a model — often billions — tuned during training. Everything the model knows lives in them. Also called weights.

item.title: Training | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The process that sets a model's parameters by exposing it to vast amounts of text and adjusting to improve next-token prediction.
item.content:encoded: The process that sets a model's parameters by exposing it to vast amounts of text and adjusting to improve next-token prediction.

item.title: Inference | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Running a trained model to generate output — what happens on every model provider request. Parameters stay fixed.
item.content:encoded: Running a trained model to generate output — what happens on every model provider request. Parameters stay fixed.

item.title: Token | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The atomic unit a model reads and writes. Roughly word-sized but not exactly. Context window size, cost, and latency all count tokens.
item.content:encoded: The atomic unit a model reads and writes. Roughly word-sized but not exactly. Context window size, cost, and latency all count tokens.

item.title: Next-token prediction | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: What the model actually does. Samples one next token from the context, appends it, and runs again. Its only mode of operation.
item.content:encoded: What the model actually does. Samples one next token from the context, appends it, and runs again. Its only mode of operation.

item.title: Non-determinism | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The same input can produce different output. A property of how models generate text and how providers serve requests.
item.content:encoded: The same input can produce different output. A property of how models generate text and how providers serve requests.

item.title: Model provider | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Whatever serves a model for inference. Usually remote (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google), but can also be local (Ollama, llama.cpp).
item.content:encoded: Whatever serves a model for inference. Usually remote (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google), but can also be local (Ollama, llama.cpp).

item.title: Harness | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Everything around the model that turns it into an agent: tools, system prompt, context-window management, permissions, hooks.
item.content:encoded: Everything around the model that turns it into an agent: tools, system prompt, context-window management, permissions, hooks.

item.title: Model provider request | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: One round-trip from the harness to the model provider. The harness sends context; the provider returns one response.
item.content:encoded: One round-trip from the harness to the model provider. The harness sends context; the provider returns one response.

item.title: Input tokens | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Tokens the harness sends on each model provider request. Billed at a lower rate than output tokens.
item.content:encoded: Tokens the harness sends on each model provider request. Billed at a lower rate than output tokens.

item.title: Output tokens | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Tokens the model generates back. Billed at a higher rate than input tokens, since they cost more compute to produce.
item.content:encoded: Tokens the model generates back. Billed at a higher rate than input tokens, since they cost more compute to produce.

item.title: Prefix cache | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The provider-side store that lets consecutive requests skip re-processing a shared prefix, billing those tokens at a lower rate.
item.content:encoded: The provider-side store that lets consecutive requests skip re-processing a shared prefix, billing those tokens at a lower rate.

item.title: Cache tokens | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Input tokens the provider has cached from a previous request via its prefix cache, billed at a much lower rate.
item.content:encoded: Input tokens the provider has cached from a previous request via its prefix cache, billed at a much lower rate.

item.title: Stateless | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Carries no information forward. The model is stateless across requests; an agent is stateless across sessions by default.
item.content:encoded: Carries no information forward. The model is stateless across requests; an agent is stateless across sessions by default.

item.title: Context | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The relevant information the agent has access to right now — what the agent knows that's pertinent to the task.
item.content:encoded: The relevant information the agent has access to right now — what the agent knows that's pertinent to the task.

item.title: Context window | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Everything the model sees on each model provider request. Finite, model-specific, the only surface through which the model perceives.
item.content:encoded: Everything the model sees on each model provider request. Finite, model-specific, the only surface through which the model perceives.

item.title: Stateful | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Carries information forward. Sessions are stateful across turns; agents can be made stateful across sessions via a memory system.
item.content:encoded: Carries information forward. Sessions are stateful across turns; agents can be made stateful across sessions via a memory system.

item.title: Agent | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: A model harnessed with tools, a system prompt, and a context window, that takes turns with a user. The model in motion.
item.content:encoded: A model harnessed with tools, a system prompt, and a context window, that takes turns with a user. The model in motion.

item.title: System prompt | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The instructions the harness prepends to every model provider request — the agent's standing brief. Usually stable across a session.
item.content:encoded: The instructions the harness prepends to every model provider request — the agent's standing brief. Usually stable across a session.

item.title: Session | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: One bounded run of interaction with an agent. Starts empty, accumulates, ends when cleared, closed, or compacted into a fresh session.
item.content:encoded: One bounded run of interaction with an agent. Starts empty, accumulates, ends when cleared, closed, or compacted into a fresh session.

item.title: Turn | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: One user message plus everything the agent does in response, up until it yields back to the user. Contains one or more provider requests.
item.content:encoded: One user message plus everything the agent does in response, up until it yields back to the user. Contains one or more provider requests.

item.title: Environment | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The world the agent acts on — anything outside the harness that the agent perceives via tool results and changes via tool calls.
item.content:encoded: The world the agent acts on — anything outside the harness that the agent perceives via tool results and changes via tool calls.

item.title: Filesystem | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: A tree of files and directories the agent reads from, writes to, and executes within — the default environment for a coding agent.
item.content:encoded: A tree of files and directories the agent reads from, writes to, and executes within — the default environment for a coding agent.

item.title: Tool | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: A function the harness exposes for the agent to call — Read, Write, Bash, Search. How an agent perceives and acts on the environment.
item.content:encoded: A function the harness exposes for the agent to call — Read, Write, Bash, Search. How an agent perceives and acts on the environment.

item.title: Tool call | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The model's output naming a tool and its arguments — just structured text. The harness has to read it and execute.
item.content:encoded: The model's output naming a tool and its arguments — just structured text. The harness has to read it and execute.

item.title: Tool result | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: What the harness sends back after executing a tool call — file contents, output, or error. The agent's only window onto the environment.
item.content:encoded: What the harness sends back after executing a tool call — file contents, output, or error. The agent's only window onto the environment.

item.title: MCP | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: A protocol for plugging external tool servers into a harness — how an agent gets tools beyond what the harness ships with.
item.content:encoded: A protocol for plugging external tool servers into a harness — how an agent gets tools beyond what the harness ships with.

item.title: Permission request | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: What the harness shows the user before executing a tool call that isn't pre-approved. The mechanism for putting a human in the loop.
item.content:encoded: What the harness shows the user before executing a tool call that isn't pre-approved. The mechanism for putting a human in the loop.

item.title: Permission mode | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The permission-gating slice of an agent mode — which tool calls trigger a permission request and which run automatically.
item.content:encoded: The permission-gating slice of an agent mode — which tool calls trigger a permission request and which run automatically.

item.title: Agent mode | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: A preset bundling a permission mode with behavioral instructions injected into the system prompt. Can flip mid-session.
item.content:encoded: A preset bundling a permission mode with behavioral instructions injected into the system prompt. Can flip mid-session.

item.title: Sandbox | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: An isolated environment the agent runs inside — container, VM, or restricted shell. Limits the blast radius of agent actions.
item.content:encoded: An isolated environment the agent runs inside — container, VM, or restricted shell. Limits the blast radius of agent actions.

item.title: Sycophancy | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Confidently agreeable model output. Caused by training that shaped the model to favor answers humans liked — including agreement.
item.content:encoded: Confidently agreeable model output. Caused by training that shaped the model to favor answers humans liked — including agreement.

item.title: Hallucination | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Confidently-wrong model output. Two flavors: factuality (invented facts) and faithfulness (drift from loaded context).
item.content:encoded: Confidently-wrong model output. Two flavors: factuality (invented facts) and faithfulness (drift from loaded context).

item.title: Parametric knowledge | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: What the model knows from training, stored in its parameters. Frozen at training time. Counterpart to contextual knowledge.
item.content:encoded: What the model knows from training, stored in its parameters. Frozen at training time. Counterpart to contextual knowledge.

item.title: Knowledge cutoff | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The date past which a model has no parametric knowledge. Post-cutoff libraries and APIs are fabrication traps unless docs are loaded.
item.content:encoded: The date past which a model has no parametric knowledge. Post-cutoff libraries and APIs are fabrication traps unless docs are loaded.

item.title: Contextual knowledge | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Facts the agent can read directly from the context right now. Counterpart to parametric knowledge.
item.content:encoded: Facts the agent can read directly from the context right now. Counterpart to parametric knowledge.

item.title: Attention relationship | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: The pairing between two tokens — meaningful pairs influence each other more than unrelated ones. A context of N tokens has ~N² of these.
item.content:encoded: The pairing between two tokens — meaningful pairs influence each other more than unrelated ones. A context of N tokens has ~N² of these.

item.title: Attention budget | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Each token has a finite amount of influence to distribute across the rest of the context. Per-token, doesn't grow when context does.
item.content:encoded: Each token has a finite amount of influence to distribute across the rest of the context. Per-token, doesn't grow when context does.

item.title: Attention degradation | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: As a session grows, each token's attention budget spreads across more competitors; signal on meaningful relationships shrinks.
item.content:encoded: As a session grows, each token's attention budget spreads across more competitors; signal on meaningful relationships shrinks.

item.title: Smart zone | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Early in a session the agent is sharp and focused. As the session grows it drifts into a dumb zone: sloppier, forgetful, more mistakes.
item.content:encoded: Early in a session the agent is sharp and focused. As the session grows it drifts into a dumb zone: sloppier, forgetful, more mistakes.

item.title: Clearing | AI Coding Dictionary
item.description: Ending the current session and starting a fresh one. The next message begins with an empty session and an empty context window.
item.content:encoded: Ending the current session and starting a fresh one. The next message begins with an empty session and an empty context window.
